Title: Carotenoid Biofortification in Field-Grown Tomato Fruits by Early Inoculation with Arbuscular Mycorrhizal Fungi
Journal: Journal of Agricultural and Food Chemistry

Pathway_Genes.Source_Species_Variety: Albenga scion in grafted plants; gene assayed in fruit tissue
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: human health-promoting bioactive compounds with antioxidant and other beneficial properties
Pathway_Genes.Pathway_Branch_or_Subpathway: plastidial isoprenoid precursor supply to fruit carotenogenesis
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: expressed in fruit pericarp; related to carotenoid biosynthesis in fruits
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: ripe fruits; about 2-fold induced in fruits of Ri plants in 2023
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: early nursery inoculation with arbuscular mycorrhizal fungus Rhizophagus irregularis under open-field conditions
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: RT-qPCR in fruits; carotenoid metabolite profiling by HPLC; literature-supported functional inference cited in paper for mutant phenotype
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: promotes carotenoid accumulation by increasing precursor supply for lycopene and β-carotene biosynthesis
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. SlGGPPS3 transcript abundance increased about 2-fold in ripe fruits from mycorrhiza-preinoculated plants. 2. The gene encodes a GGPP-producing enzyme supplying precursor for plastidial carotenoid biosynthesis. 3. Its induction is associated with increased lycopene and β-carotene accumulation in field-grown tomato fruits.
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: synthesizes geranylgeranyl diphosphate from isoprenyl diphosphate precursors to provide the immediate prenyl donor pool for carotenoid biosynthesis
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: key control point in fruit carotenoid precursor supply; supported by cited mutant evidence and induction in Ri fruits
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: no significant impact on plant height, flower number, fruit yield, weight, or size under field conditions at treatment level
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: candidate for metabolic engineering or marker-assisted enhancement of fruit carotenoids; may be useful with AM inoculation or gene stacking with PSY1
Pathway_Genes.Omics_Data: targeted carotenoid metabolite profiling by HPLC; RT-qPCR; raw data available on request
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: paper states SlGGPPS3-deficient mutants strongly reduce phytoene and lycopene based on prior literature; current study measures transcript induction rather than direct reverse-genetic manipulation in this experiment

Pathway_Genes.Source_Species_Variety: Albenga scion in grafted plants; gene assayed in fruit tissue
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: human health-promoting bioactive compounds with antioxidant and other beneficial properties
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: mainly expressed in fruits; promoted during ripening; associated with fruit pigmentation
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: ripe fruits; about 2.5-fold induced in fruits of Ri plants in 2023
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: early nursery inoculation with arbuscular mycorrhizal fungus Rhizophagus irregularis under open-field conditions
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: RT-qPCR in fruits; carotenoid metabolite profiling by HPLC; supported by prior transgenic evidence discussed in paper
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: promotes lycopene and β-carotene accumulation by enhancing entry into the carotenoid pathway
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. SlPSY1 expression increased about 2.5-fold in ripe fruits from Ri plants. 2. SlPSY1 encodes the first committed-step enzyme converting GGPP to phytoene in fruit carotenogenesis. 3. Its induction is linked to higher lycopene and β-carotene levels in field-grown tomato fruits.
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: condenses two molecules of geranylgeranyl diphosphate to form phytoene, the first committed carotenoid intermediate
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: key control point and committed-step enzyme; explicitly described as a paramount enzyme and biofortification target
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: no significant treatment-level yield penalty reported
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: strong target for carotenoid biofortification; useful for engineering or stacking with upstream precursor-supply genes and AM-based agronomic biofortification
Pathway_Genes.Omics_Data: targeted carotenoid metabolite profiling by HPLC; RT-qPCR; raw data available on request
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: paper distinguishes PSY isoforms: SlPSY1 fruit-associated, SlPSY2 photosynthetic tissues, SlPSY3 roots/apocarotenoids in mycorrhiza; current study specifically implicates SlPSY1 in fruit response

Pathway_Genes.Source_Species_Variety: Albenga scion in grafted plants; gene assayed in fruit tissue
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: β-carotene is provitamin A and health-promoting; lycopene is a health-promoting antioxidant carotenoid
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: expressed during fruit carotenogenesis; analyzed in ripe fruits
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: ripe fruits; about 3.4-fold induced in fruits of Ri plants in 2023
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: early nursery inoculation with arbuscular mycorrhizal fungus Rhizophagus irregularis under open-field conditions
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: RT-qPCR in fruits; carotenoid metabolite profiling by HPLC; supported by prior overexpression/null-mutant evidence discussed in paper
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: promotes β-carotene accumulation in ripe tomato fruits
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. SlLYC-B transcript levels increased about 3.4-fold in fruits from Ri plants. 2. The induction is interpreted as responsible for the increase in β-carotene in tomato fruits. 3. Lycopene also remained high, likely because upstream pathway genes were simultaneously induced.
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: cyclizes the linear carotene lycopene to introduce β-rings and generate β-carotene
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: key branch enzyme controlling partition from lycopene toward β-carotene
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: literature cited in paper indicates overexpression can decrease lycopene, but in this study lycopene remained elevated because upstream genes were induced; no yield penalty reported for treatment
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: valuable target for provitamin A enhancement, but should be balanced with upstream flux to avoid lycopene reduction; AM-based induction may help co-increase β-carotene and lycopene
Pathway_Genes.Omics_Data: targeted carotenoid metabolite profiling by HPLC; RT-qPCR; raw data available on request
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: paper notes prior evidence that SlLCY-B overexpression increases β-carotene and decreases lycopene, whereas null mutation strongly reduces β-carotene and increases lycopene; current field study observed elevated β-carotene without lycopene loss
